strategy_summary: The binary is a 64-bit ELF with NX enabled and no PIE. The vulnerable function reads 512 bytes into a 128-byte buffer, causing a stack overflow. We can use this to overwrite the return address. Since there is no RELRO, we can overwrite the GOT entries. We will use ROP to call system('/bin/sh') by overwriting the GOT entry of 'read' with the address of 'system'.
